title: Free Fire Max Tournament App (MVP) — Frontend Implementation Plan

requirements.summary: Create core navigation and page structure for tournaments browsing, details, creation, registrations, and profile.
acceptanceCriteria: App has a working navigation layout that lets users reach all primary sections.
Routes/pages render without placeholder errors and use English UI text.
Tournament list and details views are wired to data queries (even if seeded with initial sample data until backend is complete).

file_operations.description: Create the top-level app shell (layout + router outlet) and wire primary routes: Home (upcoming), Tournaments (browse/search), Tournament Details, Create Tournament, My Registrations, and Profile; ensure English UI text and no placeholder errors.
Create a shared layout with header + navigation links to all primary sections, and a content container for page routing.
Create the primary navigation component (links to Home, Tournaments, My Registrations, Profile, and Create Tournament when permitted) and integrate it into the layout.
Implement Home page showing upcoming tournaments; wire to tournaments query hook and display a compact list (fallback to seeded sample data if query is unavailable/empty).
Implement browse/search/filter UI for tournaments (name, region, status) and wire to a filtered tournaments query hook; navigate to Tournament Details.

Implement tournament details view (rules, region, dates, slots, status, prize details) and wire to a tournament-by-id query hook; provide sections for registration, rounds/bracket (read-only), and announcements (read-only).
Implement create tournament form UI and wire submit to backend create capability via a mutation hook; the page itself should be guarded (auth/role) elsewhere per REQ-2.

Implement My Registrations page listing the signed-in user’s registrations, with navigation back to details; wire to registrations query hook(s) (initially aggregate from tournaments + per-tournament registrations until a dedicated backend list exists).
Implement Profile page showing signed-in status and user profile name; wire to user profile query hook and provide profile setup/edit UI per REQ-2.

Create React Query hooks for listing tournaments, filtered tournaments, and fetching tournament details using the available backend actor; include safe fallbacks (e.g., seeded sample tournaments) when actor is unavailable.
Create a reusable tournament summary card used by Home and Tournaments pages to keep list UI consistent.

requirements.summary: Implement Internet Identity sign-in UX, display authenticated state, and gate organizer/admin actions and registrations behind authentication.
acceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated users can browse tournaments but cannot create/edit tournaments or register.
Authenticated users can view their profile and register for tournaments.
UI clearly indicates signed-in/signed-out state in English.

file_operations.description: Create a login/logout button using the existing Internet Identity hook to show signed-in/signed-out state; on logout, clear cached app data (React Query) as required. Use the selected "authorization" component guidance for authentication UX. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Create a small auth helper hook that derives isAuthenticated from the existing Internet Identity context and exposes convenience flags for UI gating.

Create React Query hooks for getCallerUserRole, isCallerAdmin, and getCallerUserProfile (with loading semantics that avoid profile-setup modal flash). Use the selected "authorization" component guidance for profile setup flow. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a modal/dialog that prompts newly authenticated users for a display name and saves it via saveCallerUserProfile; do not show principal id; ensure English copy. Use the selected "authorization" component guidance. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a route/page guard wrapper that blocks unauthenticated users from protected pages/actions (Create Tournament, registrations) and shows an English access message with a login call-to-action. Use the selected "authorization" component guidance for access denied UX. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a guard wrapper for organizer/admin-only sections (tournament management controls, bracket management) using role/admin checks; ensure non-organizers cannot access organizer controls in UI. Use the selected "authorization" component guidance. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Integrate AuthStatusButton in the header and conditionally render protected navigation items (e.g., Create Tournament, My Registrations) based on authentication/role state.
Wire Profile page to current user profile query; trigger ProfileSetupModal only when authenticated and profile is missing; display the profile name in English.
Wrap the page content with RequireAuth and, if organizer/admin restriction is desired for creation in MVP, RequireOrganizerOrAdmin; ensure unauthenticated users cannot create.

requirements.summary: Implement tournament registration UI flows: register with IGN/team info, view/cancel registrations, and show organizer view of entries (UI side).
acceptanceCriteria: A user can register for an open tournament and sees confirmation in the UI.
Registration is blocked when the tournament is full or registration is closed/locked.
My Registrations page shows the user’s registrations and their status.
Organizer can view a list of registered entries for their tournament.

file_operations.description: Create React Query hooks/mutations for registering a team, cancelling a registration, and loading registrations for a tournament; handle backend authorization errors gracefully in UI state.

Create a registration form component that captures captain IGN, optional team name, and squad member IGNs up to the tournament’s team size; submit via register mutation and show confirmation/error in English.

Create a details-page panel that shows the signed-in user’s current registration (if any) and allows cancel when registration is open; ensure unauthenticated users see a login prompt instead of the form.
Create an organizer-only table/list view of all registered entries for a tournament (read from registrations query) for use in the tournament management area.

Wire in RegistrationForm/MyRegistrationPanel and enforce UI gating: unauthenticated users cannot register; show registration availability rules (status, registration window, full capacity) and disable/deny actions accordingly.

Wire My Registrations to authenticated state (RequireAuth) and display registrations (initially by loading tournaments then per-tournament registrations for the user, if no dedicated backend list exists); provide cancel navigation/action when applicable.

requirements.summary: Add organizer match/round management UI and participant read-only bracket/round display (frontend scaffolding for MVP manual management).
acceptanceCriteria: Organizer can create at least one round and add matches for a tournament.
Organizer can assign registrations to matches and submit results.
Participants can view rounds/matches and results on the Tournament Details page.
Backend enforces that only the organizer (or an admin role if implemented) can modify matches/results.

file_operations.description: Create a read-only bracket/round viewer component to display rounds, matches, assignments, and results on Tournament Details; initially support local/stateful rendering with clear placeholders for backend wiring.

Create an organizer-only UI for manual round creation, match creation, assigning registrations into matches, and recording results; store draft state locally for MVP until matching backend capabilities exist; ensure it is wrapped by organizer/admin guard.
Add a 'Rounds & Results' section that shows BracketViewer to all users and shows OrganizerBracketEditor only to the organizer/admin (UI gating).
Ensure the guard can be reused for bracket management areas within Tournament Details (organizer-only modifications).

requirements.summary: Provide organizer/admin tournament management UI: edit info, change status (open/close/lock/etc.), and publish announcements shown on details.
acceptanceCriteria: Organizer can change tournament status (open/close/lock/start/complete/cancel) via UI controls.
Announcements can be created and are visible to all viewers of the tournament details.
Non-organizers cannot access organizer controls.

file_operations.description: Create mutation hooks for updating tournament fields and updating tournament status using the backend actor; surface success/error states in English and handle authorization failures gracefully.

Create an organizer/admin-only management panel for Tournament Details including: edit tournament info, status change controls, and registration/bracket lock messaging; wire status changes and edits to mutation hooks where backend capabilities exist.

Create a read-only announcements feed UI section to display tournament announcements on Tournament Details; for MVP, render from local placeholder state and prepare the component API for future backend wiring.
Create an organizer/admin-only announcement composer UI (text updates) that appends to local placeholder announcements for MVP; ensure it is guarded and clearly marked as organizer-only.
Wire OrganizerTournamentPanel into Tournament Details under organizer/admin guard; add announcements section that shows AnnouncementsFeed to all and AnnouncementComposer only to organizer/admin.

requirements.summary: Apply a coherent esports visual theme (non-blue/purple primary) using Tailwind and composed UI components without editing read-only UI component sources.
acceptanceCriteria: Theme is consistent across all primary pages and components.
Primary palette is not blue/purple; contrast and readability are acceptable.
UI uses Tailwind + existing UI components by composition (no edits to read-only component sources).

file_operations.description: Add global Tailwind base layers and define a warm/dark esports theme via CSS variables (e.g., primary in warm red/orange, avoid blue/purple) to drive shadcn-style tokens consistently across pages.
Adjust Tailwind theme extension as needed to support the esports look (typography/spacing/shadows) while keeping primary colors non-blue/purple and consistent with CSS variable tokens.
Apply consistent layout styling (spacing, background, typography) using Tailwind utility classes and composition (do not edit files under frontend/src/components/ui).
Ensure tournament cards use the global theme tokens and consistent spacing/typography for an esports tournament experience.

requirements.summary: Include required generated static images as frontend assets and use them in the UI (header/hero/branding).
acceptanceCriteria: Generated images exist under frontend/public/assets/generated with the specified filenames.
Home page and/or app header uses the generated images without routing through the backend.
Assets load correctly in the built app.

file_operations.description: Add the generated static image asset: frontend/public/assets/generated/ffm-tournament-logo.dim_512x512.png (Esports tournament shield logo with subtle flame motif, dark background friendly).
Add the generated static image asset: frontend/public/assets/generated/ffm-hero-banner.dim_1600x600.png (Wide hero banner with abstract esports arena lighting, warm accent colors).
Reference and display frontend/public/assets/generated/ffm-tournament-logo.dim_512x512.png in the app header for branding (direct static asset usage; no backend).
Reference and display frontend/public/assets/generated/ffm-hero-banner.dim_1600x600.png as the home hero/banner and ensure it loads correctly in the built app.
